title: parahebe derwentiana subsp. subglauca
intro: Parahebe derwentiana subsp. subglauca is a subspecies of flowering plant in the family Plantaginaceae, commonly known as the Tasmanian heath or blue leaf. It has been traditionally used for its medicinal properties, including its antiseptic and anti-inflammatory properties, which make it useful for treating wounds and skin irritations. The plant is also believed to have health benefits such as reducing fever and relieving respiratory issues, likely due to its expectorant properties. Herbal preparations of Parahebe derwentiana subsp. subglauca can be made into teas, infusions, or tinctures, which can be used to harness its medicinal properties.
